title: AIR Hooks Index
description: Index of hooks that run in response to agent lifecycle events. Hooks are shell commands triggered at specific points during an agent session.

propertyNames.description: Hook identifier key. Must be alphanumeric with hyphens or underscores.

Hook.description: A hook that runs a command in response to an agent lifecycle event.

id.description: Unique identifier for this hook. Must match the key in the parent object.

title.description: Human-readable display name for the hook.

description.description: Concise description of what this hook does.

event.description: Lifecycle event that triggers this hook.

command.description: Shell command to execute when the event fires.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command.

env.description: Environment variables for the hook process.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time in seconds before the hook is killed.

matcher.description: Regex pattern to match against event data. Hook only fires when matched.
